notes: Community Origin optional section is absent, acceptable for internal story.

executor @dev is allowed under validate-next-story rules.
quality_gate @ux-design-expert is not allowed under validate-next-story rules, which list @architect, @dev, and @pm.
For a broad code/features story, @dev with @architect would match the formal mapping; for design/UI-only work, split the story and use @ux-design-expert with @dev.

issues: Scope combines visual design, data model, UI components, database, API routes, storage, workers, agents, asset providers, approvals, webhooks, exports, integrations, and full tests in one increment.
Concrete schemas, endpoints, files, environment variables, storage paths, worker contracts, retry rules, and dependency order are not summarized in the story.
References point mostly to whole documents instead of exact sections/tasks.
Developer must read and reconcile the full FBR-Design PRD/SPEC/tasklist to implement safely.
Referenced brand-kit webhook contracts conflict between DOCUMENTO_GERAL.md section 11.5 and PRD_SPEC_TASKLIST.md TASK-DS16.
Test files, fixtures, mocks, HMAC vectors, worker fixtures, storage fixtures, and validation steps are not mapped to specific acceptance criteria or source tasks.

blocking_issues: Story scope is too large; source docs already separate this into DS-V01..DS-V05, DS-D01..DS-D02, DS-C01..DS-C06, DS-A01, and TASK-DS01..TASK-DS17 across ordered implementation tasks.
Executor assignment is invalid because @ux-design-expert is not an allowed quality_gate in validate-next-story rules.
Dev Notes are not self-contained enough to satisfy Article IV for implementation.

Cross-module brand-kit webhook contract conflicts across referenced sources: DOCUMENTO_GERAL.md section 11.5 uses brand_kit.updated with nested data fields, while PRD_SPEC_TASKLIST.md TASK-DS16 uses brand_kit_updated with a different payload shape.
File locations are broad path groups and may not match the current repo workspace structure.
Acceptance criteria require matching the full PRD_SPEC_TASKLIST and broad test classes without embedding the specific pass/fail conditions.
should_fix_issues: Split or narrow the story into implementable increments following source dependency order.
Map each acceptance criterion to exact source tasks and verification steps.

Summarize the Brand Kit JSON schema, 8 auto-review rules, format taxonomy, database/RLS schema, API contracts, upload validation, worker interfaces, asset finder cascade, export behavior, HMAC rules, retry/debounce rules, storage paths, and environment variables.
Resolve whether production paths are apps/modules/design and backend/api/design or repo-aligned apps/portal/src paths before development.
